sessions.lever: blocked-lane triage — confirmed coupling to consumer functions

reviewer.evidence: No candidate edit to review — spu_InitEx body is HEAD's clean pure-C, unchanged. Blocker is file-scope, not in this function.

remaining_gap.summary: spu_InitEx body is COMPLETED-C-clean (floor 0, 0 rules, 0 cheat-asm) but CANNOT close independently: queue done requires full-build SHA1==oracle, which fails because two file-scope globals it shares (D_800A28A0, D_800A2CD4) are declared `extern volatile` (main.c:48/50). Removing volatile drops 40 bytes / 10 insns from the link — all in the CONSUMER functions coli_HitPauseKatana_2 (main.c:2017) and func_8008AAD4 (main.c:2326), NOT in spu_InitEx. spu_InitEx only WRITES these globals to 0; its own bytes are volatile-independent.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: None (0 regfix/asmfix). The cheat is the two `extern volatile` decls — a CSE-defeat coercion on plain game-state globals, FORBIDDEN per inline-asm-policy / split-read-defeats-hoist (the latter explicitly flags coli_HitPauseKatana_2's volatile-based match as NO LONGER VALID, needs re-derivation). Closing spu_InitEx requires the consumers to byte-match WITHOUT volatile.

next_hypotheses: These globals are NOT IRQ-touched: the ONLY writers are spu_InitEx (init-to-0) and the |= updates inside coli_HitPauseKatana_2 / func_8008AAD4 — none installed as an interrupt/VSync/CD callback. So the legitimate-volatile-interrupt-touched carve-out does NOT apply (allowlist correctly omits both symbols). Do NOT add them to volatile_extern_allowlist.txt.

Re-derive coli_HitPauseKatana_2 + func_8008AAD4 so GCC re-reads `D_800A2CD4 & 1` per-branch WITHOUT volatile. split-read-defeats-hoist lever #1 (duplicate the read into arms) + #2 (direct-symbol index in the known arm) are ALREADY applied (main.c:2024-2028) but do NOT defeat the repeated `if (D_800A2CD4 & 1)` reads across the switch on their own — that is the remaining structural gap.

Coupled scope: func_8008AAD4 also carries register-asm pins (register u32 X asm("a1"/"a2"/"a3"/"t0"), main.c:2327-2330) which are themselves cheats — closing this cluster is a coordinated 3-function pure-C re-derivation, not a single-function pass.

If no no-volatile structural lever exists for the consumers, the cluster is a canonical-asm-authorization candidate — but the consumers are NOT obviously hand-coded asm (they're ordinary C control flow), so canonical-asm is unlikely to be the right answer; the structural lever should be found instead.

rejected_forms.form: Remove `volatile` from D_800A28A0 + D_800A2CD4 decls, leave consumers as-is
rejected_forms.reason: Full build FAILS: -40 bytes (10 insns) in coli_HitPauseKatana_2 / func_8008AAD4 — GCC CSEs the repeated `if (D_800A2CD4 & 1)` reads that target re-loads. Proves volatile is load-bearing for the CONSUMERS, so spu_InitEx is coupled.

rejected_forms.form: Add D_800A28A0 / D_800A2CD4 to volatile_extern_allowlist.txt (IRQ carve-out)
rejected_forms.reason: Does NOT qualify: legitimate-volatile-interrupt-touched requires (1) an IRQ-handler writer cited + (2) a spin-wait/double-read/IRQ-loop-bound use-site. Neither holds — writers are ordinary spu funcs, use-sites are plain `if (G & 1)` switch tests. Adding to allowlist would be an unauthorized self-grant.
